posts.title: Why sin⁻¹x is not 1/sin x
posts.summary: sin²x means (sin x)², so it's natural to read sin⁻¹x as (sin x)⁻¹ = 1/sin x. That's wrong — and the reason untangles a notation clash that confuses almost every pre-calc student.

posts.title: Why √x is never negative — but x² = 9 gives ±3
posts.summary: The square root symbol always returns a non-negative number. So where does the ± come from when we solve x² = 9? A clean walk-through of the distinction that trips up a lot of students.
